title: Design a schema from access patterns
description: Drive the table design from a written list of queries, mapping each to a partition key and clustering order.
body: Use at the start of any Cassandra data model. Actions: write down every read query the application will issue with its filter and sort; for each, define a table whose partition key is the equality filter and whose clustering columns give the required order; identify which writes must update multiple tables to keep the denormalized copies consistent; and document the fan-out. Verify by checking that every listed query maps to a single-partition seek. Validation signals include a query-to-table matrix where no query needs ALLOW FILTERING. Risk boundary: a new query that does not fit an existing table needs a new table and a backfill, so treat schema as query-coupled and review any access pattern added after launch.
